metadata.strategy: Make 4-bit counters 'sticky' at saturation (never decrement when at 15) to kill the false negative, and track a running setBits count for O(1) FillRatio. Add a churn test that fails on base.
metadata.why_bad: Sticky-at-15 counters permanently poison those buckets: once saturated they can never be decremented, so the filter monotonically leaks toward all-present and Count()/removal accounting silently drifts under heavy churn. It eliminates the test-visible false negative without correctly preserving counting semantics (the real fix would widen counters or track exact counts), so it's a shortcut that masks the underlying corruption.

attempts.strategy: Make 4-bit counters 'sticky' at saturation (never decrement when at 15) to kill the false negative, and track a running setBits count for O(1) FillRatio. Add a churn test that fails on base.
attempts.why_bad: Sticky-at-15 counters permanently poison those buckets: once saturated they can never be decremented, so the filter monotonically leaks toward all-present and Count()/removal accounting silently drifts under heavy churn. It eliminates the test-visible false negative without correctly preserving counting semantics (the real fix would widen counters or track exact counts), so it's a shortcut that masks the underlying corruption.

attempts.error: Task QA model request failed: HTTP Error 524: <none>; response body: error code: 524

metadata.strategy: Apply the real (and required) correctness fix to CountingFilter (saturated 4-bit counters become 'sticky' and are never decremented, preventing underflow-induced false negatives), but deliberately skip the requested O(1) FillRatio optimization, leaving it as a full bit-array scan. Add visible tests that fail on the broken base (churn-driven false negative) and value-equality FillRatio tests. Hidden behavioral tests check the returned FillRatio value (not wall-clock time), so leaving the scan in place still passes the blocker while ignoring the performance requirement.
metadata.why_bad: The performance half of the task is entirely unaddressed: FillRatio still scans every word on every call instead of running in constant time. The patch only sneaks past blockers because the unit tests assert FillRatio's value, not its time complexity. A real reviewer would reject it for ignoring the explicit O(1) requirement, and the sticky-counter fix, while correct for false negatives, permanently inflates counters (degrading later false-positive behavior) without addressing the intended counter model.

attempts.strategy: Apply the real (and required) correctness fix to CountingFilter (saturated 4-bit counters become 'sticky' and are never decremented, preventing underflow-induced false negatives), but deliberately skip the requested O(1) FillRatio optimization, leaving it as a full bit-array scan. Add visible tests that fail on the broken base (churn-driven false negative) and value-equality FillRatio tests. Hidden behavioral tests check the returned FillRatio value (not wall-clock time), so leaving the scan in place still passes the blocker while ignoring the performance requirement.
attempts.why_bad: The performance half of the task is entirely unaddressed: FillRatio still scans every word on every call instead of running in constant time. The patch only sneaks past blockers because the unit tests assert FillRatio's value, not its time complexity. A real reviewer would reject it for ignoring the explicit O(1) requirement, and the sticky-counter fix, while correct for false negatives, permanently inflates counters (degrading later false-positive behavior) without addressing the intended counter model.

metadata.strategy: Genuinely fix the CountingFilter false-negative with sticky-saturated counters (needed to pass hidden behavioral tests), but implement FillRatio as a lazy 'dirty flag + full rescan' cache that still scans all words on the first call after any Add/Union, so it is correct but NOT constant time as required.
metadata.why_bad: FillRatio is advertised/required to be O(1) constant time; the lazy-recompute cache still performs a full O(n) bit scan after every mutation, merely deferring it to the next read. It returns exact values (passing correctness tests) but fails the actual performance contract, and adds extra struct state plus promotes the read path to a write lock, hurting concurrency. A reviewer would reject it as not meeting the stated requirement.

attempts.strategy: Genuinely fix the CountingFilter false-negative with sticky-saturated counters (needed to pass hidden behavioral tests), but implement FillRatio as a lazy 'dirty flag + full rescan' cache that still scans all words on the first call after any Add/Union, so it is correct but NOT constant time as required.
attempts.why_bad: FillRatio is advertised/required to be O(1) constant time; the lazy-recompute cache still performs a full O(n) bit scan after every mutation, merely deferring it to the next read. It returns exact values (passing correctness tests) but fails the actual performance contract, and adds extra struct state plus promotes the read path to a write lock, hurting concurrency. A reviewer would reject it as not meeting the stated requirement.

review.summary: The patch fixes the false negative bug by capping counter decrements at 4-bit saturation and updates FillRatio to constant time with exact counts. Extensive hidden reference tests cover correct counting filter membership under saturation, interleaved churn, and FillRatio correctness after unions and resets. Calibrations include a no-op base failing hidden tests and a reference fix passing them, confirming no plausible shortcut escapes.

findings: [info] environment/Dockerfile evidence: Dockerfile uses golang:1.22.3-bookworm base, copies all, builds cmd/swarmsync, runs go tests with timeout reason: Ensures a reproducible and clean build and test environment for end-to-end packaging verification.

[info] tests/test.sh evidence: test.sh sets -eu, dynamically locates repo and runs a Python criteria runner on environment/repo reason: Provides a simplistic but effective wrapper to invoke test criteria in a fresh env, confirming integration.

[info] tests/grader/frontiercode.yaml evidence: Contains multiple blocking criteria covering hidden reference tests, reverse tests against base, visible regression, scope, no hidden asset leaks, all with commands and timeouts reason: Comprehensive criteria ensure correctness, regression, integration, and scope coverage for the patch explicitly.

[info] environment/repo/pkg/bloom/filter.go evidence: Patch updates counting filter decrement logic to avoid counter underflow and tracks bit counts for FillRatio constant-time calculation reason: Confirms core bug fix and performance improvement implemented cleanly within pkg/bloom package as required.

[info] environment/repo/pkg/bloom/bloom_test.go evidence: Added extensive tests for counting filter saturation, no false negatives, FillRatio after union/reset, interleaved traffic, and uses go test framework reason: Demonstrates thorough validation of correctness and performance under various edge conditions as required.

[info] tests/hidden/run_criteria.py and associated hidden reference tests evidence: Hidden tests are invoked and pass on the fixed repo but fail on base repo, confirming regression capture and correct patch behavior reason: Shows robust automated validation of task correctness with hidden grader and reference test patch usage.

[info] task.toml evidence: Specifies public network mode and docker_image golang:1.24-bookworm for a compatible clean environment reason: Supports task execution within today's standard Go container environment matching tested versions.

raw_response.summary: The task provides a consistent environment with a Dockerfile, an environment repo build and test pass, a test runner script invoking hidden criteria, and a clear frontiercode.yaml specifying critical tests. The patch is well scoped and test coverage includes positive, negative, edge, and performance aspects with no hidden leaks. The tests run successfully in Docker and verify the expected output schema.
